qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow via deeply nested protobuf messages during parsing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a stack overflow vulnerability in the C++ Protobuf parsing implementation (libprotobuf), while the advisory describes denial-of-service vulnerabilities in the PHP Protobuf library (Packagist: google/protobuf). The advisory root causes are: (1) negative varints accepted without validation causing infinite loops in PHP CodedInputStream, and (2) recursion limit bypass in PHP due to a logic bug in incrementRecursionDepthAndPushLimit. The finding describes a C++ issue where recursive parsing exhausts stack despite recursion_budget_ limit. These are different language implementations with different vulnerable code, different root causes (PHP input validation bug vs C++ stack depth management), and different attack vectors (negative varints/deep recursion in PHP vs deep nesting in C++).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes denial-of-service vulnerabilities in the Protobuf PHP library (Packagist: google/protobuf) caused by (1) negative varints accepted without validation leading to infinite loops/worker exhaustion, and (2) deep recursion limit bypass due to a logic bug in PHP CodedInputStream. The stack_overflow_nested_message finding describes a C++ stack overflow issue in libprotobuf's recursive parsing, which is a different language implementation with different vulnerable code locations and different root cause. The finding does not identify the PHP-specific vulnerable code (php/src/Google/Protobuf/Internal/CodedInputStream.php) or the PHP-specific root causes (negative varint validation, recursion_limit decrement bug).
evidence: Advisory references: GitHub issues #24159 and #25067 (PHP-specific issues)
Issue #24159 root cause: PHP CodedInputStream::readVarint32() accepts negative varints without validation
Issue #25067 root cause: PHP incrementRecursionDepthAndPushLimit() has logic bug preventing recursion limit enforcement
stack_overflow_nested_message finding: C++ implementation (src/google/protobuf/io/coded_stream.h, parse_context.h, wire_format_lite.h)
stack_overflow_nested_message PoC: builds C++ library (cmake, g++, libprotobuf.a)
Different language implementations: PHP vs C++ protobuf libraries
Different root causes: PHP input validation bug and recursion limit logic bug vs C++ stack depth management
